skill.name: brainstorming
skill.description: Guides early-stage product and engineering ideas into validated designs through questions, alternative approaches, and structured documentation templates.
skill.summary: Turn rough ideas into clear, validated design options before implementation.
skill.author: ArieGoldkin

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows they are Markdown examples, placeholders, or ordinary product-design language. No executable scripts, credential file access, network reconnaissance, C2 behavior, or prompt injection attempts were found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keywords In Markdown Text
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines are headings, placeholders, design-process words, or authentication example prose. I found no cryptographic implementation, weak hash usage, encryption routine, or executable code at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files are Markdown guidance and examples. The matches are semantic words such as design text and option labels, not code paths that perform cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Backticks Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported backtick execution locations are fenced Markdown examples, inline file references, or template snippets. They are not Ruby, shell, or any executable command runner.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context shows Markdown fences around diagrams and example prompts. There is no script file, interpreter invocation, shell command construction, or user-controlled execution vector.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Browser Storage And Cookie Discussion
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines discuss session storage, local storage, cookies, and secure authentication architecture as design alternatives. They do not read browser credential databases or access local browser storage files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The auth example asks users to choose a storage strategy and recommends httpOnly cookies. It contains no filesystem paths to browser profiles and no code that extracts stored credentials.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance And C2 Terms In Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines use ordinary planning terms such as dependencies, network failure, SOC2, and cross-tab access. I found no network scanning, command-and-control endpoint, beaconing, or system inventory behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms appear inside templates and design examples. The files provide planning prompts and do not contain commands or instructions to probe systems or contact external infrastructure.

content.user_title: Plan Better Product And Engineering Designs
content.value_statement: Early ideas often fail because requirements and trade-offs stay vague. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through questions, options, and validation before implementation starts.
actual_capabilities: Asks one clarifying question at a time to define purpose, constraints, and success criteria.
Compares two or three architecture or product approaches with explicit trade-offs.
Presents designs in short sections and pauses for validation after each section.
Uses included design document and decision matrix templates for structured outputs.
Provides example sessions for authentication storage and real-time dashboard planning.
limitations: Does not implement code or replace a separate implementation plan.
Depends on user answers for accurate requirements and constraints.
Does not validate technical claims against live systems or current vendor documentation.
Provides planning structure, not legal, security, or compliance certification.

use_cases.title: Clarify A New Feature
use_cases.description: Turn a rough feature request into goals, constraints, success criteria, and a preferred design direction.

use_cases.title: Compare Architecture Options
use_cases.description: Evaluate several implementation approaches before the team commits to one technical direction.

use_cases.title: Prepare Design Documentation
use_cases.description: Create a structured design document and decision matrix before starting implementation work.

prompt_templates.title: Start From A Rough Idea
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an idea for a product feature. Help me clarify the goal, users, constraints, and success criteria before we plan implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Explore Implementation Choices
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me compare two or three ways to build this feature. Show the trade-offs, complexity, risks, and when each option makes sense.

prompt_templates.title: Validate A Proposed Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this proposed design through the brainstorming workflow. Ask clarifying questions, identify missing constraints, and suggest safer alternatives.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Decision Matrix
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me build a weighted decision matrix for these options. Define criteria, score each option, explain the recommendation, and list revisit triggers.

output_examples.input: I need authentication, but I am unsure where to store session data.
output_examples.output: The skill asks about storage needs, security level, and success criteria. It then compares cookies, server sessions, and token approaches before presenting a validated design.

output_examples.input: Build a real-time analytics dashboard for user activity.
output_examples.output: The skill clarifies metrics, scale, and decision goals. It compares polling, server-sent events, and WebSockets, then outlines data flow, resilience, and metrics.

output_examples.input: We have three possible approaches and need to choose one.
output_examples.output: The skill creates criteria, compares trade-offs, assigns weighted scores, explains the recommendation, and records when the decision should be revisited.

best_practices: Answer each clarifying question with concrete constraints and real success criteria.
Ask for at least two alternatives before choosing an implementation direction.
Validate each design section before moving into implementation planning.
anti_patterns: Skipping discovery and asking for code before the design is validated.
Treating the first proposed approach as final without comparing alternatives.
Using generic success criteria that cannot guide trade-off decisions.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It turns early ideas into clearer designs through questions, approach comparisons, and incremental validation.

faq.question: Does it write production code?
faq.answer: No. It is intended for discovery and design before implementation planning or coding begins.

faq.question: Can it help with technical architecture?
faq.answer: Yes. It compares architecture options and explains trade-offs, complexity, risks, and suitable use cases.

faq.question: What templates are included?
faq.answer: It includes a design document template and a decision matrix template for structured planning outputs.

faq.question: Is it useful for non-engineering ideas?
faq.answer: Yes. The questioning and trade-off process can support product, workflow, and documentation decisions.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
